# agentops_channel_route (Resource)

A route on an `agentops_channel` that forwards matching events to a target agent or workflow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "agentops_channel_route" "deploys" {
  channel_id  = agentops_channel.alerts.id
  rule_type   = "keyword"
  target_type = "agent"
  target_id   = "agent_01hxyz"
  priority    = 10

  match = jsonencode({
    keyword = "deploy"
  })
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `channel_id` (String) ID of the channel this route belongs to. Changing this forces a new route.
- `priority` (Number) Evaluation priority; lower values are evaluated first.
- `rule_type` (String) Match rule type. One of `private`, `channel`, `keyword`, `mention`.
- `target_id` (String) ID of the target agent or workflow.
- `target_type` (String) Kind of target the route forwards to (e.g. `agent`, `workflow`).

### Optional

- `input` (String) Input payload template forwarded to the target, as a JSON object.
- `is_default` (Boolean) Whether this is the channel's default route.
- `is_enabled` (Boolean) Whether the route is enabled.
- `match` (String) Match criteria for the route, as a JSON object.

### Read-Only

- `account_id` (String) Account that owns the route.
- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `id` (String) Route identifier.
- `updated_at` (String) Last-update timestamp.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Channel routes are imported as "channel_id/route_id".
terraform import agentops_channel_route.deploys chn_01hxyz/rt_01hxyz
```
